mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Add an id_token_hint parameter to the logout provider URL for OIDC provider
mission.description: Add an id_token_hint parameter to the logout provider URL for OIDC provider. Requested by 21+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
Comply with the specified recommendations on https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-rpinitiated-1_0.html#RPLogout
Okta example: https://developer.okta.com/docs/reference/api/oidc/#logout

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add an id_token_hint parameter to the logout provider URL…
steps.description:
Reading through this, I think we need to have a wider discussion about single sign out, it's not something we have intended to support in the past but we could possibly start doing so.

IIRC OIDC providers can provide a sign out URL as part of their well known endpoint. We should enable the OIDC
```yaml
Application: Web
Domain: dev-1234.okta.com
Grant Types: Auhtorization Code, Refresh Token
Login redirect URIs: https://myapp.com/oauth2/callback	
Logout redirect URIs: https://myapp.com/oauth2/sign_in
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add an id_token_hint parameter to the logout provider URL…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Reading through this, I think we need to have a wider discussion about single sign out, it's not something we have intended to support in the past but we could possibly start doing so.

IIRC OIDC providers can provide a sign out URL as part of their well known endpoint. We should enable the OIDC provider to leverage this when we are signing people out of OAuth2 Proxy.
codeSnippets:
Application: Web
Domain: dev-1234.okta.com
Grant Types: Auhtorization Code, Refresh Token
Login redirect URIs: https://myapp.com/oauth2/callback	
Logout redirect URIs: https://myapp.com/oauth2/sign_in

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
